developer.name: Harshit
developer.learningGoal: Understand every line of code — no copy-paste without understanding
developer.previousHabit: Heavy reliance on Gemini CLI for code generation — actively breaking this habit

section_1_executive_summary.title: Executive Summary
section_1_executive_summary.description: Onelink is a Link in Bio SaaS platform that allows users to create a personalized public profile page at a custom URL (e.g., onelink.app/harshit). Users can add, reorder, and manage links to their social profiles, portfolio, and projects — all accessible from a single, shareable URL.
section_1_executive_summary.purpose: Fullstack portfolio piece built to demonstrate end-to-end development capability — from database design and authentication to dynamic routing and analytics.
key_objectives: Build a production-grade fullstack SaaS application for portfolio showcase
Demonstrate competency in Next.js 16, TypeScript, Prisma v7, and NextAuth v4
Deploy a live, working product with real authentication and database
Learn every concept hands-on — understanding every line of code written
success_metrics: Working Google OAuth login flow end-to-end
Users can create profile, add links, and share public URL
Deployed to Vercel — accessible on the internet
Can explain every part of the codebase in an interview

section_2_project_overview.title: Project Overview
section_2_project_overview.name: Onelink
section_2_project_overview.tagline: One link for everything you are
target_audience: Students and developers who want to share multiple links from one place
Creators and freelancers maintaining a digital identity
Recruiters and portfolio reviewers — primary demo audience

value_proposition.tagline: One link for everything you are
points: Single URL that works as a digital identity hub
Click analytics to understand audience engagement
Multiple visual themes — dark, light, gradient

framework.name: Next.js
framework.reason: Full-stack in one project. Dynamic routing, API routes, SSR built-in. No need for separate Express server.

language.name: TypeScript
language.reason: Type safety — catch errors at write time, not runtime. Prevents a whole class of bugs.

styling.name: Tailwind CSS
styling.note: No tailwind.config.ts in v4 — configuration done via globals.css using CSS variables
styling.reason: Utility-first, fast to build, highly readable class names

display.name: Syne
display.usage: Logo, hero text, headings, button labels

authentication.reason: Battle-tested v4 is more stable than v5 beta. More control than Clerk. Better for learning auth internals.

rejected_alternatives.name: Clerk
rejected_alternatives.reason: Too abstracted — less control, harder to explain internals in interview

rejected_alternatives.name: NextAuth v5 / Auth.js
rejected_alternatives.reason: Still in beta. Different config syntax would disrupt mid-project.

rejected_alternatives.name: Better Auth
rejected_alternatives.reason: Accidentally installed and removed. New and unproven.

database.reason: Already familiar with Neon from other projects. Free tier sufficient.

rejected_alternatives.name: Supabase
rejected_alternatives.reason: Neon already familiar — no reason to switch

orm.name: Prisma
orm.v7_breaking_change: DATABASE_URL moved from schema.prisma datasource block to prisma.config.ts. Requires dotenv package.
orm.reason: Type-safe DB queries in JavaScript. Schema-first approach. Excellent DX.

dependencies.name: dotenv
dependencies.purpose: Load .env in prisma.config.ts — required by Prisma v7

removed_packages.name: better-auth
removed_packages.reason: Accidentally installed during auth library exploration. Removed — using NextAuth v4.

folder_structure.app/layout.tsx: Root layout — wraps every page. Contains Syne + DM Mono font setup and metadata.
folder_structure.app/globals.css: Global styles, Tailwind @import, CSS variables for --font-syne and --font-dm-mono
folder_structure.app/login/page.tsx: Custom login page — split screen layout with animated canvas background
folder_structure.app/setup/page.tsx: Username setup page for new users — planned
folder_structure.app/[username]/page.tsx: Public profile page — dynamic catch route — planned
folder_structure.app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts: NextAuth catch-all route — exports GET and POST handlers
folder_structure.app/api/links/route.ts: GET all links + POST create link — planned
folder_structure.lib/auth.ts: NextAuth configuration — authOptions, providers, adapter, callbacks
folder_structure.lib/prisma.ts: Prisma client singleton — prevents multiple DB connections during hot reload
folder_structure.types/next-auth.d.ts: TypeScript module augmentation — extends Session and JWT interfaces with id and username
folder_structure.prisma/schema.prisma: Database schema — 5 models: User, Account, Session, VerificationToken, Link
folder_structure.prisma.config.ts: Prisma v7 config — contains datasource URL reference (project root)
folder_structure.middleware.ts: Route protection — runs before every request on matched routes
folder_structure..env: DATABASE_URL — used by Prisma CLI only

auth_flow: 4. NextAuth redirects to Google OAuth consent screen
6. NextAuth verifies token, creates User + Account rows in Neon via Prisma Adapter
7. JWT created with user id and username — stored in encrypted cookie
8. Middleware checks: has username? Yes → /dashboard. No → /setup
middleware_flow: 1. Every request to /dashboard/* or /setup hits middleware first
2. getToken() reads JWT from request cookie
3. If not logged in + accessing /dashboard → redirect to /login
4. If logged in + no username + not on /setup → redirect to /setup
5. If logged in + has username + on /setup → redirect to /dashboard
6. All other cases → NextResponse.next() — let request through
link_click_tracking_flow: 2. Page fetches all active links for that username from DB
3. User clicks a link on the public profile
5. Prisma: UPDATE Link SET clicks = clicks + 1 WHERE id = [id]
6. User is redirected to the actual link URL
dashboard_link_crud_flow: 2. GET /api/links fetches all their links ordered by order field
3. User adds new link → POST /api/links with { title, url }
4. User edits link → PATCH /api/links/[id] with updated fields
5. User reorders via drag-and-drop → PATCH multiple links with new order values

section_5_database.title: Database Design
important_notes: Always use migrate dev in development — never db push (no history)
Run migrate after every schema.prisma change
Each migration creates a timestamped SQL file in prisma/migrations/
Prisma v7: DATABASE_URL lives in prisma.config.ts, not schema.prisma

prisma_attribute_reference.@unique: Single field must be unique across all rows
prisma_attribute_reference.@@unique([a,b]): Combination of fields must be unique
prisma_attribute_reference.@default(cuid()): Auto-generates a unique ID when row is created
prisma_attribute_reference.@default(now()): Auto-sets current timestamp when row is created
prisma_attribute_reference.onDelete: Cascade: When parent deleted, auto-delete all child rows

User.purpose: Main user table — every signup creates a row here

fields.name: id
fields.note: Auto-generated unique ID using cuid algorithm

fields.name: name
fields.note: Display name — pulled from Google OAuth profile

fields.name: email
fields.note: Unique email — no two users can share same email

fields.name: emailVerified
fields.note: Required by NextAuth for email verification flow

fields.name: image
fields.note: Profile photo URL — will be Cloudinary URL

fields.name: username
fields.note: Custom slug — becomes /username public route. Set on /setup page.

fields.name: bio
fields.note: Short profile description shown on public page

fields.name: theme
fields.note: Selected visual theme — dark | light | gradient

fields.name: views
fields.note: Total profile page view count

fields.name: createdAt
fields.note: Account creation timestamp

fields.name: accounts
fields.note: Relation — one user can have many OAuth accounts

fields.name: sessions
fields.note: Relation — one user can have many sessions

fields.name: links
fields.note: Relation — one user can have many links

Account.purpose: Managed automatically by NextAuth — stores OAuth provider tokens. Never touched directly in app code.

fields.name: userId
fields.note: Foreign key — references User.id

fields.name: type
fields.note: OAuth type

fields.name: provider
fields.note: e.g. 'google'

fields.name: providerAccountId
fields.note: User's unique ID on that provider

fields.name: refresh_token
fields.note: OAuth refresh token

fields.name: access_token
fields.note: OAuth access token

fields.name: expires_at
fields.note: Token expiry timestamp

unique_constraints.explanation: Prevents connecting same OAuth account twice. Allows same user to connect both Google AND GitHub because those have different provider values.
unique_constraints.example_blocked: Connecting same google account twice to same user
unique_constraints.example_allowed: Connecting github account to same user who already has google connected

Session.purpose: Managed by NextAuth — stores active user sessions.
Session.note: Project uses JWT strategy so sessions are in cookies. This table is populated by adapter for compatibility.

VerificationToken.purpose: Managed by NextAuth — stores temporary tokens for email verification flows.

Link.purpose: Core app model — every link a user adds to their Onelink profile page.

fields.name: id
fields.note: Auto-generated unique ID

fields.name: title
fields.note: Display name e.g. My GitHub, Portfolio

fields.name: url
fields.note: The actual destination URL

fields.name: clicks
fields.note: Incremented every time someone clicks this link on public profile

fields.name: order
fields.note: Position index — used for drag-and-drop reordering. Lower = higher on page.

fields.name: isActive
fields.note: Toggle link visibility on public profile

fields.name: userId
fields.note: Foreign key — which user owns this link

fields.name: user
fields.note: Cascade delete — if user deleted, all their links auto-delete

section_6_authentication.title: Authentication

auth_options.adapter: PrismaAdapter(prisma as any) — cast as any due to type mismatch between @auth/prisma-adapter and NextAuth v4
auth_options.session_strategy: jwt — session stored in cookie, not database

jwt.purpose: Runs when token is created or refreshed
jwt.logic: If user just logged in: fetch their username from DB, attach id + username to token
jwt.code_summary: if (user) { token.id = user.id; const dbUser = await prisma.user.findUnique({ where: { id: user.id }, select: { username: true } }); token.username = dbUser?.username; } return token;

session.purpose: Runs when session is read by the app
session.logic: Attach id and username from JWT token to session.user so components can access them
session.code_summary: session.user.id = token.id as string; session.user.username = token.username as string | null; return session;

route_handler.explanation: [...nextauth] is a catch-all dynamic route that matches all paths under /api/auth/ including /signin, /signout, /callback/google, /session, /csrf
route_handler.exports: export { handler as GET, handler as POST }
route_handler.why_both_methods: NextAuth uses GET for fetching session and page redirects. Uses POST for login submissions and OAuth callbacks.

google_oauth_setup_steps: Navigate to APIs and Services then Credentials
Click Create Credentials then OAuth Client ID
Copy Client ID and Client Secret to .env.local

.env.local.NEXTAUTH_URL: http://localhost:3000 in development. Production URL after deploy.
.env.local.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID: From Google Cloud Console OAuth credentials
.env.local.GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET: From Google Cloud Console OAuth credentials

environment_variables.security_note: Both .env and .env.local are in .gitignore — never committed to GitHub

type_extensions.purpose: NextAuth v4 does not include id or username in default Session type. Module augmentation extends them.

section_7_middleware.title: Middleware
section_7_middleware.location: Project root — same level as app/ folder
section_7_middleware.purpose: Runs before every request on matched routes. Acts as security guard.
section_7_middleware.token_reading: getToken() from next-auth/jwt reads JWT from request cookie

key_variables.token: JWT token from cookie — contains id and username
key_variables.isAuth: !!token — true if user is logged in

routing_logic.condition: IS logged in AND NO username AND NOT on /setup
routing_logic.action: Redirect to /setup — new user must pick username

routing_logic.condition: IS logged in AND HAS username AND IS on /setup

login.directive: use client — canvas animation requires browser APIs
login.layout_type: Split screen — branding left, login card right
left_panel: Logo top-left — onelink with accent color on link
Hero headline: One link. Everything you. with gradient on Everything
3 feature bullet points with colored dots (indigo, violet, pink)

card_contents: Solid white Google OAuth button with FcGoogle icon

background.resize_handling: window resize event listener updates canvas width and height

design_iterations.feedback: Disliked cyan, card felt off, too many elements
Better but overall layout feels wrong

design_iterations.direction: Split screen with 4 palette options shown
design_iterations.feedback: Liked split screen. Chose Option A Indigo/Violet

setup.trigger: Middleware redirects new users here when they have no username
features: Real-time availability check via debounced API call
Validation: alphanumeric + hyphens, 3-20 chars, must be unique
On submit: PATCH /api/profile with { username } then redirect to /dashboard

link_editor_features: Add new link — form with title and URL fields
Edit existing link — inline edit on click
Drag and drop reorder — using dnd-kit library
Toggle link active/inactive via isActive boolean
dashboard.live_preview: Real-time preview panel showing how public profile will look as user edits

features: Bar or line chart using Recharts
Fetches user and active links from DB by username
Profile photo, display name, bio at top
All active links as styled buttons
Click tracking: POST /api/links/[id]/click then redirect to URL
Returns 404 if username does not exist
Delete account — cascades to delete all links

section_9_api_routes.title: API Routes
architecture_notes: All routes use Next.js App Router — export named HTTP method functions
Auth-protected routes use getServerSession(authOptions) to verify login
No separate Express server — all backend inside Next.js API routes

completed.description: NextAuth: fetch session, redirect to signin, handle OAuth provider pages
NextAuth: login submission, OAuth callback, signout

planned.description: Fetch all links for logged-in user ordered by order field
Create new link for logged-in user
Update specific link — verify ownership first
Delete specific link — verify ownership first
Increment click count — called from public profile on each click

planned.body: Partial<{ name, bio, username, theme, image }>

section_10_design_system.title: Design System
section_10_design_system.aesthetic: Obsidian — dark, premium, minimal. Inspired by Linear, Vercel, Raycast.

accent_light.usage: Logo link color, subtle indigo tints

rejected_palettes.name: Cyan
rejected_palettes.reason: Too neon — did not fit premium aesthetic

concepts.explanation: A JS function that returns JSX. JSX uses className not class because class is reserved in JavaScript.
Folder name = URL path. app/page.tsx = /. app/dashboard/page.tsx = /dashboard. app/[username]/page.tsx = /harshit.
[username] catches ONE segment. [...nextauth] catches ALL segments from that point forward.
Create (POST), Read (GET), Update (PATCH), Delete (DELETE) — the four basic DB operations.
One User has many Links. Child (Link) stores parent id as foreign key. Parent (User) has array field links Link[].
When parent row deleted, auto-delete all child rows. Without it, deleting a User leaves orphan Link rows.
@unique = one field unique. @@unique([a,b]) = combination unique. Used in Account to prevent same OAuth account twice.
npx prisma migrate dev reads schema, creates tables in DB, saves SQL migration file. Like git commit for database.
DATABASE_URL moved from schema.prisma to prisma.config.ts. Requires dotenv package.
Store PrismaClient on globalThis to survive Next.js hot reloads. Without this each file save creates a new DB connection.
Exports handler as both GET and POST. Next.js requires exact HTTP method function names.
jwt callback attaches custom data to token. session callback exposes token data to React components.
Declaration files extend existing library types via module augmentation without modifying the library.
Runs before every request. Used for auth checks and redirects. matcher limits which routes trigger it.
Converts any value to boolean. !!null = false. !!'value' = true. Clean way to check existence.
Opens VS Code in current directory. . always refers to current directory in terminal.

issues.issue: npx auth triggered Better Auth CLI
issues.root_cause: better-auth remained in node_modules after uninstall — package.json was clean but node_modules was not
issues.fix: npm uninstall better-auth then rmdir /s /q node_modules && npm install for full clean reinstall
issues.lesson: node_modules can contain packages not in package.json — clean reinstall resolves ghost packages

issues.issue: Prisma migrate failed — url no longer supported in schema.prisma
issues.error: Error code P1012 — datasource property url is no longer supported
issues.root_cause: Prisma v7 breaking change — DATABASE_URL moved from schema.prisma to prisma.config.ts
issues.fix: Created prisma.config.ts with defineConfig and datasource.url. Removed url from schema.prisma datasource block. Installed dotenv.
issues.do_not: Do not add url back to schema.prisma — that is intentionally removed in v7

issues.issue: PrismaAdapter TypeScript error TS2345 — type mismatch
issues.root_cause: Type incompatibility between @auth/prisma-adapter and NextAuth v4 internal Prisma types
issues.fix: PrismaAdapter(prisma as any) — cast as any
issues.note: Standard community workaround for this version combination

issues.issue: Property id does not exist on session.user — TS2339
issues.root_cause: NextAuth v4 default Session type only includes name, email, image
issues.fix: Created types/next-auth.d.ts extending Session and JWT interfaces with id and username

issues.root_cause: Route file imported lib/auth before lib/auth.ts was created
Missing flex-shrink-0 on right panel — flex was expanding it to fill available space
issues.fix: Added w-[340px] flex-shrink-0 to right panel container

issues.root_cause: Prisma v7 dev tools depend on older hono and lodash
issues.resolution: Intentionally ignored — vulnerabilities are in Prisma CLI tools, not app code
issues.action: Do NOT run npm audit fix --force — it downgrades Prisma to v6.19 which is a breaking change

Link scheduling — active between certain dates only
QR code generator for profile URL

section_14_development_guidelines.title: Development Guidelines
learning_rules: Understand before typing — concept explained before writing code
Type all code manually — no copy-paste without understanding
Stop and ask immediately when confused
No AI code generation tools — only code you can explain
Be able to explain every decision in an interview
code_conventions: use client directive required for files using browser APIs or React hooks
API routes export named HTTP method functions (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE)
All DB queries via lib/prisma.ts — never instantiate PrismaClient directly
Environment variables via process.env — never hardcode secrets
git_workflow: Commit after each working feature or fix
database_workflow: Run npx prisma migrate dev after EVERY schema change
Never use npx prisma db push — skips migration history
Verify tables in Neon dashboard after migrations
Use npx prisma studio for visual inspection

steps.task: Finalize login page pixel-perfect layout matching Option A preview
Build /setup page — username input, validation, save to DB, redirect
Build /dashboard — link CRUD with add, edit, delete, toggle, drag-and-drop
Build /[username] public profile — fetch by username, display links, 404 handling
Add POST /api/links/[id]/click route for click tracking
Build analytics dashboard — views, clicks, Recharts chart
Implement multi-theme system — stored per user, applied on public profile
Add Cloudinary for profile photo uploads
Deploy to Vercel — configure env vars, update Google OAuth redirect URI for production
